# Rust API client for iqm-client

> [!CAUTION]
>  Please note that this IQM Server API is currently in development and may be subject to changes. For reference,
our legacy API documentation can be found [here](?api=integration). However, we advise against integrating with the legacy version,
as it will be deprecated soon. Additionally, access to the legacy integration API now requires IP whitelisting by IQM Resonance.


The **IQM Server API** is an HTTP interface that provides programmatic access to IQM Quantum Computer resources.
It is used by the official IQM client tools, but external parties can also use it for custom integrations.

The IQM Server API replaces the following legacy APIs:

* Station API (On-Prem only)
* Resonance API (Cloud only)
* Cocos API (On-Prem and Cloud)

## API Versioning

The API follows this versioning scheme:

```plain
/api/<version>/<resource>
```

The latest API version can be accessed using the alias `latest`:

```plain
/api/latest/<resource>
```

Available versions can be queried with:

```plain
GET /api/versions
```

#### Major changes (breaking)

When a breaking change is introduced, a new API version is published, and the old one remains available
for existing clients. Clients are strongly encouraged to pin to a fixed version for stability. The `latest`
alias should only be used for development and testing.

#### Minor changes (non-breaking)

IQM Server may introduce backwards-compatible changes to existing versions. Clients are expected to handle
these gracefully.

Data Sent by the Server

* JSON objects: new properties may be added to existing schemas. Clients must ignore unknown properties
  (implicit `\"additionalProperties\": true`).
* Protobuf messages: new fields may be added. Clients must ignore unknown fields, as described in the
  [Protobuf language guide](https://protobuf.dev/programming-guides/proto3/#updating).
* Schema narrowing: for example, if a schema was `error_code: int | string`, it may later be restricted
  to `error_code: int` without affecting runtime behavior.
* Enums and `one_of` fields: variants may be removed as long as runtime behavior is preserved.

Data Sent by the Client

* JSON objects: Clients must not send extra properties. Although the server may ignore them, new optional
  input properties added in the future could conflict and cause requests to fail.
* Protobuf messages: Clients must not send unknown fields for the same reason.

#### Endpoints

The list of endpoints is not guaranteed to be static. New endpoints may be added to existing versions.

### Deprecation

When a new version is released, older versions are automatically marked as deprecated.
If a client uses a deprecated version, the server will include this response header:

```
IQM-API-Deprecated: true
```

Deprecated versions may be removed after a deprecation period, so clients should migrate promptly.

The server may also mark individual operations as deprecated. In this case, responses will include:

```
IQM-API-Operation-Deprecated: true; breaking=<version>
```

Where `<version>` is the API version in which the operation will stop working. If this header is absent,
upgrading to a newer version should not break the operation.

## Authentication

All requests to the IQM Server API require an API Token. The token must be passed as a Bearer token in
the `Authorization` header:

```
curl -H \"Authorization: Bearer <token>\" https://<server-url>/api/<version>/<resource>
```

API tokens are available from the server web dashboard under the profile menu. They are personal and scoped
to a single workspace. If a user has multiple workspaces, each requires its own token.



## Overview

This API client was generated by the [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech) project.  By using the [openapi-spec](https://openapis.org) from a remote server, you can easily generate an API client.

- API version: v1
- Package version: v1
- Generator version: 7.22.0
- Build package: `org.openapitools.codegen.languages.RustClientCodegen`

## Installation

Put the package under your project folder in a directory named `iqm-client` and add the following to `Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:

```
iqm-client = { path = "./iqm-client" }
```
